key_insight: V alloying turns MoTe2 into a stable magnetic monolayer with strong, tunable valley polarization that can be amplified by electric field and biaxial strain.
engineering_relevance: Mo0.75V0.25Te2 is a promising 2D valleytronic platform for low-power transistors and sensors because its valley splitting can be externally controlled.
method: First-principles DFT calculations on V-alloyed MoTe2 monolayer (Mo0.75V0.25Te2).
Assessed structural stability, electronic structure, and SOC-induced valley splitting.
Evaluated tuning via transverse electric field and biaxial strain.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports no imaginary phonon modes for stability and quantified valley splittings of 37.3 meV (CB) and 78.2 meV (VB), rising to 132.8 meV under electric field and 160.8 meV under tensile strain.
watch_item: The results are computational and depend on idealized alloying, field, and strain conditions, so experimental synthesis and disorder effects may alter the predicted valley polarization.
paper_title: Strain- and Electric-Field-Tunable Valley Polarization in Mo0.75V0.25Te2(Mo3VTe8) for Valleytronic Application
